reasoning: The question requires Shakhriyar Mamedyarov to gain at least 1% (+27.46 points) in his FIDE standard classical rating, extracted from the Wikipedia 'FIDE rankings' page. FIDE rankings are officially updated only on the 1st of each month, which means his official rating on June 29 will almost certainly remain identical to his June 22 reading (2746). For subsequent months, jumping ~28 points to >2773 is practically impossible for a 40-year-old Super-GM currently out of the Top 10, lacking major active top-level tournament action (and often seen coaching or playing smaller events). While a negligible probability tail exists for him catching a massive lucky streak later in the year, he fundamentally lacks the volume points and stability to jump a whole 28 Elo over such short time horizons. Probabilities are strictly bounded to minimum allowed thresholds based on prompt instructions, scaled up slightly for extended time horizons to account for variance.
